sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.59 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: One can show that any extension of shape${ Z}^d.\, { SL}_d({ Z})$is residually finite, unless possibly$d=3$or$d=5$. Are there in fact any extensions of this shape that fail to be residually finite when$d=5$? As shown in (P.\,R.\,Hewitt, Groups/St.\,Andrews'93 in Galway, Vol.\,2$($London Math. Soc. Lecture Note Ser., 212), Cambridge Univ. Press, 1995, 305--313), there is an extension of${ Z} ^3$by$...
